triggers.name: Apache Kafka: Unclean leader election detected
triggers.description: Unclean leader elections occur when there is no qualified partition leader among Kafka brokers. If Kafka is configured to allow an unclean leader election, a leader is chosen from the out-of-sync replicas, and any messages that were not synced prior to the loss of the former leader are lost forever. Essentially, unclean leader elections sacrifice consistency for availability.

triggers.name: Apache Kafka: One or more partitions have no leader
triggers.description: Any partition without an active leader will be completely inaccessible, and both consumers and producers of that partition will be blocked until a leader becomes available.

triggers.name: Apache Kafka: There are partitions under the min ISR
triggers.description: The Under min ISR partitions metric displays the number of partitions, where the number of In-Sync Replicas (ISR) is less than the minimum number of in-sync replicas specified. The two most common causes of under-min ISR partitions are that one or more brokers is unresponsive, or the cluster is experiencing performance issues and one or more brokers are falling behind.

triggers.name: Apache Kafka: There are under replicated partitions
triggers.description: The Under replicated partitions metric displays the number of partitions that do not have enough replicas to meet the desired replication factor. A partition will also be considered under-replicated if the correct number of replicas exist, but one or more of the replicas have fallen significantly behind the partition leader. The two most common causes of under-replicated partitions are that one or more brokers is unresponsive, or the cluster is experiencing performance issues and one or more brokers have fallen behind.

https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/rate-limits-for-the-rest-api?apiVersion=2022-11-28

triggers.name: Hadoop: NameNode: Cluster has volume failures
triggers.description: HDFS now allows for disks to fail in place, without affecting DataNode operations, until a threshold value is reached. This is set on each DataNode via the dfs.datanode.failed.volumes.tolerated property; it defaults to 0, meaning that any volume failure will shut down the DataNode; on a production cluster where DataNodes typically have 6, 8, or 12 disks, setting this parameter to 1 or 2 is typically the best practice.

triggers.name: Hadoop: ResourceManager: Cluster has no active NodeManagers
triggers.description: Cluster is unable to execute any jobs without at least one NodeManager.

triggers.name: Hadoop: ResourceManager: Cluster has unhealthy NodeManagers
triggers.description: YARN considers any node with disk utilization exceeding the value specified under the property yarn.nodemanager.disk-health-checker.max-disk-utilization-per-disk-percentage (in yarn-site.xml) to be unhealthy. Ample disk space is critical to ensure uninterrupted operation of a Hadoop cluster, and large numbers of unhealthyNodes (the number to alert on depends on the size of your cluster) should be quickly investigated and resolved.

triggers.expression: min(/HashiCorp Nomad Server by HTTP/nomad.server.raft.leader.lastContact,5m) >= {$NOMAD.SERVER.LEADER.LATENCY} and nodata(/HashiCorp Nomad Server by HTTP/nomad.server.raft.leader.lastContact,5m) = 0
triggers.description:
The nomad.raft.leader.lastContact metric is a general indicator of Raft latency which can be used to observe how Raft timing is performing and guide infrastructure provisioning.

triggers.name: Jira Data Center: Mail: Error queue contains one or more items
triggers.description:
A mail queue attempts to resend items up to 10 times. If the operation fails for the 11th time, the items are put into an error mail queue.
You can remove items from the error mail queue in one of the following ways:
  - Manually clear the whole error queue.
  - Manually resend all items from the error queue to a mail queue.
You should pay attention to the cases where an error mail queue item gets back to an error mail queue after you resend the items manually. These cases might indicate permanent performance issues.

Locations can be set by few ways:

2. by location name (for example: Riga)
3. by location ID. Link to the list of city ID: http://bulk.openweathermap.org/sample/city.list.json.gz
4. by zip/post code with a country code (for example: 94040,us)
A few locations can be added to the macro at the same time by `|` delimiter.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Excessive configuration cache usage
triggers.expression: max(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent/rcache.buffer.pused,10m)>{$ZABBIX.SERVER.UTIL.MAX:"configuration cache"}
triggers.description: Consider increasing `CacheSize` in the `zabbix_server.conf` configuration file.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Failed to fetch stats data
triggers.description: Zabbix has not received statistics data for `{$ZABBIX.SERVER.NODATA_TIMEOUT}`.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Wrong template assigned
triggers.expression: last(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent/server_check)=0
triggers.description: Check that the template has been selected correctly.

items.name: Trend function cache, % of unique requests

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Excessive value cache usage
triggers.expression: max(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent/vcache.buffer.pused,10m)>{$ZABBIX.SERVER.UTIL.MAX:"value cache"}
triggers.description: Consider increasing `ValueCacheSize` in the `zabbix_server.conf` configuration file.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Zabbix value cache working in low-memory mode
triggers.expression: last(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent/vcache.cache.mode)=1
triggers.description: Once low-memory mode has been switched on, the value cache will remain in this state for 24 hours, even if the problem that triggered this mode is resolved sooner.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Version has changed
triggers.expression: last(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent/version,#1)<>last(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent/version,#2) and length(last(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent/version))>0
triggers.description: Zabbix server version has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Excessive vmware cache usage
triggers.expression: max(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent/vmware.buffer.pused,10m)>{$ZABBIX.SERVER.UTIL.MAX:"vmware cache"}
triggers.description: Consider increasing `VMwareCacheSize` in the `zabbix_server.conf` configuration file.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Excessive history index cache usage

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Utilization of unreachable poller processes is high
triggers.expression: avg(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/process.unreachable_poller.avg.busy,10m)>{$ZABBIX.SERVER.UTIL.MAX:"unreachable poller"}
triggers.description: Indicates potential performance issues with the unreachable poller, which may affect monitoring efficiency and response times.

items.name: Utilization of vmware collector data collector processes, in %

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Utilization of vmware collector processes is high
triggers.expression: avg(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/process.vmware_collector.avg.busy,10m)>{$ZABBIX.SERVER.UTIL.MAX:"vmware collector"}
triggers.description: Indicates potential performance issues with the vmware collector, which may affect monitoring efficiency and response times.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Excessive configuration cache usage
triggers.expression: max(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/rcache.buffer.pused,10m)>{$ZABBIX.SERVER.UTIL.MAX:"configuration cache"}
triggers.description: Consider increasing `CacheSize` in the `zabbix_server.conf` configuration file.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Failed to fetch stats data
triggers.expression: nodata(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/rcache.buffer.pused,{$ZABBIX.SERVER.NODATA_TIMEOUT})=1
triggers.description: Zabbix has not received statistics data for `{$ZABBIX.SERVER.NODATA_TIMEOUT}`.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Wrong template assigned
triggers.expression: last(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/server_check)=0
triggers.description: Check that the template has been selected correctly.

items.name: Trend function cache, % of unique requests

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Excessive value cache usage
triggers.expression: max(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/vcache.buffer.pused,10m)>{$ZABBIX.SERVER.UTIL.MAX:"value cache"}
triggers.description: Consider increasing `ValueCacheSize` in the `zabbix_server.conf` configuration file.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Zabbix value cache working in low-memory mode
triggers.expression: last(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/vcache.cache.mode)=1
triggers.description: Once low-memory mode has been switched on, the value cache will remain in this state for 24 hours, even if the problem that triggered this mode is resolved sooner.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Version has changed
triggers.expression: last(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/version,#1)<>last(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/version,#2) and length(last(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/version))>0
triggers.description: Zabbix server version has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Excessive vmware cache usage
triggers.expression: max(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/vmware.buffer.pused,10m)>{$ZABBIX.SERVER.UTIL.MAX:"vmware cache"}
triggers.description: Consider increasing `VMwareCacheSize` in the `zabbix_server.conf` configuration file.

items.name: Number of values synchronized with the database per second

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Excessive history cache usage
triggers.expression: max(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/wcache.history.pused,10m)>{$ZABBIX.SERVER.UTIL.MAX:"history cache"}
triggers.description: Consider increasing `HistoryCacheSize` in the `zabbix_server.conf` configuration file.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Excessive history index cache usage
triggers.expression: max(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/wcache.index.pused,10m)>{$ZABBIX.SERVER.UTIL.MAX:"index cache"}
triggers.description: Consider increasing `HistoryIndexCacheSize` in the `zabbix_server.conf` configuration file.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: Excessive trends cache usage
triggers.expression: max(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/wcache.trend.pused,10m)>{$ZABBIX.SERVER.UTIL.MAX:"trend cache"}
triggers.description: Consider increasing `TrendCacheSize` in the `zabbix_server.conf` configuration file.

triggers.name: Zabbix server: More than 100 items have been missing data for over 10 minutes
triggers.expression: min(/Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active/zabbix.stats[{$ZABBIX.SERVER.ADDRESS},{$ZABBIX.SERVER.PORT},queue,10m],10m)>100
triggers.description: Indicates potential issues with network connectivity, agent failures, or unresponsive monitored resources that require attention.

template: Zabbix server health by Zabbix agent active

triggers.name: Zookeeper: Too many queued requests
triggers.description: Number of queued requests in the server. This goes up when the server receives more requests than it can process.

triggers.name: Zookeeper: Server mode has changed
triggers.expression: last(/Zookeeper by HTTP/zookeeper.server_state,#1)<>last(/Zookeeper by HTTP/zookeeper.server_state,#2) and length(last(/Zookeeper by HTTP/zookeeper.server_state))>0
triggers.description: Zookeeper node state has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Zookeeper: Failed to fetch info data
triggers.description: Zabbix has not received data for items for the last 10 minutes

triggers.name: Zookeeper: Version has changed
triggers.expression: last(/Zookeeper by HTTP/zookeeper.version,#1)<>last(/Zookeeper by HTTP/zookeeper.version,#2) and length(last(/Zookeeper by HTTP/zookeeper.version))>0
triggers.description: Zookeeper version has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.
